mission: Choose the canonical Vecells UK-hosted runtime topology baseline covering cloud-region posture, tenant isolation, acting-scope fencing, workload-family ownership, trust-zone boundaries, gateway surface splits, and resilience posture without binding the product to a speculative cloud vendor SKU.

chosen_baseline.browser_rule: Browsers terminate only at public edge and published gateway surfaces; no direct data or integration reachability.
chosen_baseline.tenant_rule: Platform, multi-tenant, and cross-organisation work must carry explicit blast radius and exact acting scope tuples.
chosen_baseline.resilience_rule: Writable posture freezes whenever runtime publication, acting scope, or resilience tuples drift.

region_option_scorecard.label: Single UK region with same-region recovery only
region_option_scorecard.region_posture: One UK region, backup in the same region, no second live-ready region role.
region_option_scorecard.tenant_model: Shared runtime, shared control plane, shared storage partitions.
region_option_scorecard.rejection_reason: Fails the requirement to make disaster-recovery blast radius explicit across two UK-hosted region roles.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

region_option_scorecard.label: Dual UK region, shared control plane, shared runtime
region_option_scorecard.region_posture: Primary and secondary UK regions with largely shared compute tiers and shared data partitions.
region_option_scorecard.tenant_model: Shared runtime and shared storage partitions with strong logical fences.
region_option_scorecard.rejection_reason: Improves region posture, but tenant blast radius remains too broad for governance, support, and release watch tuples.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 114 - tenant and acting context drift

region_option_scorecard.label: Shared control plane and shared runtime with strong tenant isolation
region_option_scorecard.region_posture: Dual UK region, but tenant work stays inside the same compute fleet.
region_option_scorecard.tenant_model: Shared compute fleet, strict tenant tuple and cache partitioning.
region_option_scorecard.rejection_reason: Still leaves release blast radius and support/governance cross-tenant paths too dependent on policy discipline inside one shared hot path.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene

region_option_scorecard.label: Fully isolated per-tenant deployment baseline
region_option_scorecard.region_posture: Dual UK regions per tenant with fully isolated runtime stacks.
region_option_scorecard.tenant_model: Per-tenant deploy, per-tenant stores, per-tenant control surfaces.
region_option_scorecard.rejection_reason: Over-rotates into operational duplication and weakens the shared assurance, release, and simulator posture expected by the corpus.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

region_option_scorecard.label: Hybrid baseline with shared platform services and tenant-scoped runtime/data slices
region_option_scorecard.region_posture: Dual UK region provider-neutral baseline with one primary and one secondary region role.
region_option_scorecard.tenant_model: Shared edge, shell-delivery, assurance, and release control planes plus tenant-scoped command/projection/data slices.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene

trust_zones.purpose: TLS termination, origin policy, rate limiting, DDoS and attack-surface filtering.
trust_zones.notes: This is the first browser-contacting band, but it is not the browser-addressable compute boundary.

trust_zones.purpose: Static asset, manifest, and shell bundle delivery for published UI shells.
trust_zones.notes: This band serves shell assets and manifests but never becomes a browser-visible compute proxy.

trust_zones.purpose: The only browser-addressable compute boundary via published GatewayBffSurface contracts.
primary_controls: No direct adapter or raw data access
trust_zones.notes: Patient, staff, support, operations, and governance surfaces split here when session policy, tenant isolation, or downstream workload set changes.

trust_zones.purpose: Command and projection execution inside declared bounded-context seams.
trust_zones.notes: This band carries tenant-scoped command and projection work and may only be reached through the published gateway boundary.

trust_zones.purpose: External adapter egress, callback ingress, and transport correlation.
trust_zones.notes: Gateways never reach this band directly in the chosen baseline.

trust_zones.purpose: Relational, projection, object, queue, cache, and audit storage classes.
primary_controls: Tenant tuple on every key or stronger scope tuple
trust_zones.notes: Every record, event, queue message, cache key, and object prefix stays tenant-scoped here.

trust_zones.purpose: Policy compilation, runtime publication, immutable audit, detection, trust verdicts, and resilience control.
primary_controls: Immutable evidence and trust freeze verdicts
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme
trust_zones.notes: This band may inform runtime posture, but it may not be bypassed or reconstructed from dashboard-local state.

workload_family_templates.notes: No PHI assembly, no adapter egress, and no runtime truth inference beyond the published boundary state.
Delivers compiled shells and manifests, but never becomes a mutation, query join, or supplier egress plane.
Owns authoritative mutations, leases, and outbox initiation. Browser reads never terminate here.
Owns audience-safe read models, live deltas, and recovery-aware completeness truth.
Owns all supplier egress and callback ingress. Gateways never call this family directly in the chosen baseline.
Never browser-addressable. Every row, message, cache key, and object prefix must carry tenant scope.

workload_family_templates.display_name: Assurance, security, and resilience control plane
workload_family_templates.notes: Publishes runtime truth, trust, redaction, recovery, and resilience posture and keeps operator controls frozen when tuples drift.

trust_zone_boundaries.notes: Static shell delivery crosses the edge without carrying PHI-bearing browser compute.
This is the only browser-origin compute hop in the topology baseline.
Gateways may read projections and dispatch commands, but the tenant tuple and runtime binding must survive this hop unchanged.

source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene
trust_zone_boundaries.notes: Support replay, operations drilldown, and governance routes consume this boundary when they expose evidence or control posture.
All primary stores, projections, queues, caches, and object prefixes stay in this zone and remain non-browser-addressable.
All external side effects begin from durable outbox or queue handoff rather than browser or gateway direct egress.
Command, projection, and runtime publication consume assurance verdicts here rather than inferring trust posture from local health.
Callback and transport results may settle state only through published checkpoint and correlation rules.
Adapter failures may degrade dependent slices without collapsing unrelated families.

source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene
trust_zone_boundaries.notes: Audit, runtime publication, and resilience evidence stores sit behind this boundary.

region_resilience_matrix.uk_residency_rule: All production, preprod restore, and disaster-recovery data paths remain UK-hosted. No DR promotion may target a non-UK region.

regions.usage: Normal write path for managed environments.
Warm standby, restore rehearsal target, failover target, and replay target for declared store classes.
Developer-only local execution; not authority for production or DR posture.

environment_region_bindings.notes: Local builds may emulate topology but are never production authority.
Ephemeral preview posture in the primary UK role only.
Simulator-backed verification ring before restore rehearsal.
Restore rehearsal and failover validation ring.
Secondary region stays warm and promotable only under declared authority.

store_classes.name: FHIR-capable relational store
store_classes.primary_posture: Primary-region writable, tenant-partitioned logical schemas or keys.
store_classes.secondary_posture: Warm replica in secondary UK region; promotion requires restore proof, publication parity, and scope-tuple refresh.
store_classes.failover_user_posture: command_halt then read_only until tuple refresh and projection readiness converge

store_classes.name: Object and artifact storage
store_classes.primary_posture: Primary-region writes with immutable object manifests and malware posture.
store_classes.secondary_posture: Cross-region UK replication with manifest parity verification before promotion.
store_classes.failover_user_posture: summary_only or governed handoff until artifact parity recovers

store_classes.name: Event bus, queue, and outbox checkpoints
store_classes.primary_posture: Primary-region active dispatch and callback correlation.
store_classes.secondary_posture: Checkpointed durable mirror; replay from durable checkpoints only.
store_classes.failover_user_posture: integration_queue_only and command_halt for affected routes

store_classes.name: Projection read store
store_classes.secondary_posture: Warm or rebuildable copy; readiness verdict required before live claims.

store_classes.name: Append-only audit and assurance ledger
store_classes.secondary_posture: Replicated or dual-written UK evidence path with continuity verification.
store_classes.failover_user_posture: diagnostic_copy_only until resilience tuple becomes exact

store_classes.name: Ephemeral cache and shell continuity cache
store_classes.primary_posture: Region-local disposable cache keyed by tenant tuple.
store_classes.secondary_posture: Cold or disposable; never authoritative during failover.
store_classes.failover_user_posture: recompute from projection or drop to recovery_only

queue_and_outbox_posture: Every external effect originates from an outbox or durable queue checkpoint in the primary region.
Secondary-region activation replays only durable checkpoints and authoritative correlation state.
Gateways never bypass queue or outbox discipline for failover expediency.

failover_authorities.role: ResilienceOrchestrator proposes restore or failover posture from exact runtime truth.
Operations drilldown may execute declared actions only while the resilience tuple and watch tuple remain exact.
Governance must approve platform or multi-tenant blast-radius widening, rollback stand-down, and exceptional recovery scope.

degraded_user_postures.reason: ScopedMutationGate freezes mutation on tuple or runtime drift.

degraded_user_postures.posture: masked replay visible, restore controls frozen
degraded_user_postures.reason: InvestigationScopeEnvelope remains visible, but restore authority halts on drift.
ReleaseWatchTuple and resilience tuple decide authority, not dashboard-local state.

source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

decision_log.title: Adopt provider-neutral dual-UK-region roles
decision_log.rationale: The bootstrap requires UK-hosted cloud infrastructure, and resilience work requires explicit primary and secondary UK roles without speculative vendor SKU names.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

decision_log.title: Choose hybrid shared platform services with tenant-scoped runtime and data slices
decision_log.rationale: This keeps release, assurance, and edge controls shared while making tenant blast radius machine-readable in command, projection, and data paths.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene

decision_log.title: Treat gateway surfaces as explicit per-surface contracts, not one generic BFF
decision_log.rationale: The blueprint forbids one broad BFF whenever tenant isolation, trust-zone boundary, downstream family set, or recovery profile changes.

decision_log.title: Forbid browser-to-integration direct reachability in the baseline
decision_log.rationale: Although the runtime blueprint names integration as a downstream family type, this baseline keeps browser routes off the adapter plane to preserve control-plane clarity and validator simplicity.

decision_log.title: Bind restore and failover authority to resilience tuples, not dashboards
decision_log.rationale: Restore authority was called out as fragmented; the baseline therefore keeps operations drilldown and resilience controls tuple-bound and gateway-split from general boards.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 112 - restore authority fragmenting across runbooks and dashboards
phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme

decision_log.title: Normalize topology drift into published recovery dispositions
decision_log.rationale: Browser shells, staff workspaces, support replay, and governance controls all keep context but freeze or degrade through the declared recovery contracts rather than generic errors.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 120 - degraded-mode fragmentation across shells and channels

assumptions.summary: Provider-neutral region labels remain the canonical baseline because the repository does not pin a cloud vendor or region code.
The local ring remains `nonprod_local`; all managed production, disaster-recovery, and restore-proof paths stay UK-hosted.
Deferred Phase 7 embedded surfaces and future-optional standalone assistive controls still need topology rows now so later work cannot invent hidden boundaries.

risks.summary: Exceptional tenancy or regulator demands may still force a later tenant-specific deployment variant, so this baseline keeps that option visible but not default.
source_refs: phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9H. Tenant governance, config immutability, and dependency hygiene

risks.summary: Support, operations, and governance surfaces depend on exact tuple refresh discipline; stale local shells would re-open the acting-scope drift defects the forensic audit closed.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 114 - tenant and acting context drift
